name: Concrete Volume Calculator
description: Industrial-grade calculator for estimating concrete volume, material waste, and cost with lean and Six Sigma principles.

inputs.label: Length
inputs.businessContext: Length of the concrete slab or structure in meters. Based on ISO 9001 measurement standards.

inputs.label: Width
inputs.businessContext: Width of the concrete element in meters. Typical for industrial floors and foundations.

inputs.label: Depth / Thickness
inputs.businessContext: Thickness of the concrete pour in meters. Common range for slabs: 0.1–0.3 m.

inputs.label: Shape
inputs.businessContext: Geometric shape of the pour area. Affects volume calculation and waste factors.

inputs.label: Waste Factor
inputs.businessContext: Expected material waste due to spillage, over-excavation, or formwork leakage. Lean target < 3%.

inputs.label: Compression Strength Class
inputs.businessContext: Concrete strength class per EN 206. Higher strength increases cost and reduces volume yield slightly.

inputs.label: Reinforcement Ratio
inputs.businessContext: Steel reinforcement as percentage of concrete volume. Typical range 0.5–3% for structural slabs.

inputs.label: Labor Rate
inputs.businessContext: Hourly labor cost for concrete placement and finishing. Based on regional wage data.

inputs.label: Material Cost per m³
inputs.businessContext: Cost of ready-mix concrete delivered to site. Includes additives if specified.

inputs.label: Use Concrete Pump
inputs.businessContext: Indicates if a concrete pump is required for placement. Adds cost and reduces waste.

rules.message: For L-shaped pours, minimum waste factor should be 5% due to formwork complexity.
High strength concrete in thin sections may cause thermal cracking. Consider lower strength or increase depth.
Reinforcement ratio above 3.5% may cause congestion and placement issues per ACI 318.

thresholds.message: Volume exceeds 500 m³. Consider phased pouring to control thermal and shrinkage cracking.
Total cost exceeds $100,000. Recommend value engineering review.
Waste volume > 10 m³. Investigate process waste reduction using DMAIC.

formulas.name: Base Geometric Volume
formulas.description: Calculates the nominal volume based on shape.
formulas.formula: if shape == 'rectangular': length * width * depth; if shape == 'circular': pi * (length/2)^2 * depth; if shape == 'L-shaped': (length * width * depth) + (0.5 * length * width * depth); if shape == 'trapezoidal': ((length + width)/2) * depth * width

formulas.name: Adjusted Volume with Waste
formulas.description: Applies waste factor to base volume.

formulas.name: Steel Reinforcement Volume
formulas.description: Volume of steel based on reinforcement ratio.

formulas.name: Labor Hours Estimate
formulas.description: Estimated labor hours based on volume and complexity.

formulas.name: Total Cost
formulas.description: Sum of material and labor costs.

primary.label: Total Concrete Volume Required
primary.description: Net concrete volume including waste, excluding steel displacement.

baseVolume.label: Base Geometric Volume
baseVolume.description: Volume before waste and steel adjustment.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Waste Factor
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: High waste factor increases volume and cost. Lean target < 3%.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Reinforcement Ratio
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: Higher ratio reduces net concrete volume but increases steel cost and placement complexity.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Shape Complexity
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: Non-rectangular shapes increase waste and labor. L-shaped and trapezoidal have higher loss.

suggestedActions.action: Implement lean construction techniques to reduce waste factor below 3%.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Save up to 2% of total concrete volume.

suggestedActions.action: Review structural design to minimize reinforcement ratio without compromising strength.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Reduce steel volume and cost by 5-10%.

suggestedActions.action: Use concrete pump for complex shapes to reduce placement waste.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Reduce waste by 1-2% and labor hours by 10%.

suggestedActions.action: Order concrete in full truck batches (6 m³) to minimize leftover and cost.
